title: Rinorea monticola
taxonomy: Rinorea monticola is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Violaceae. It belongs to the genus Rinorea and is native to the mountainous regions of Southeast Asia. The plant is a member of the order Malpighiales and is commonly found in tropical rainforests. Rinorea monticola is closely related to other species of Rinorea, which are also found in the Violaceae family. The exact classification of Rinorea monticola is as follows: kingdom: Plantae, clade: Angiosperms, clade: Eudicots, clade: Rosids.

intro: Rinorea monticola, also known as the Himalayan hill grape, is a climbing plant species native to the Himalayan region, belonging to the family Violaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including fever, rheumatism, and respiratory issues, due to its reported anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Rinorea monticola include alkaloids, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
